notes: All 18 steps + 1 prerequisite completed in single iteration. Installed opentimelineio, implemented export_otio (EDL->OTIO conversion with clips, transitions, metadata, hash), detect_otio_changes (structural comparison), and full error handling. 36 tests pass including integration test with real DB.

summary: Ensure opentimelineio is installed in the dev environment (pip install opentimelineio) so tests can use the real library for round-trip validation.; Write failing tests for public API surface: OtioExportError importable and is Exception subclass, export_otio importable with correct signature (edl, output_path, db -> Path), detect_otio_changes importable with correct signature, __all__ exports all three names.; Create autopilot/plan/otio_export.py with OtioExportError exception class, export_otio stub (raises NotImplementedError), detect_otio_changes stub, and __all__ list. Make step-1 tests pass.; Write failing tests for timecode-to-RationalTime conversion: _tc_to_rational_time correctly converts HH:MM:SS.mmm to RationalTime at given fps. Test multiple timecodes (00:00:00.000, 00:01:30.500, etc.) and fps values (24, 30, 60).; Implement _tc_to_rational_time helper using validator.timecode_to_seconds and otio.opentime.RationalTime.from_seconds(). Make step-3 tests pass.; Write failing tests for basic clip conversion: given a minimal EDL with 1 clip and a mock DB (with file_path and fps), export_otio produces a .otio file. Verify: file exists, can be read back by otio.adapters.read_from_file, timeline has 1 video track, track has 1 clip, clip has correct source_range (in/out timecodes as RationalTime), clip has ExternalReference with target_url pointing to source file_path.; Implement core export_otio: create Timeline, create video Track, convert EDL clips to otio.schema.Clip with MediaReference (ExternalReference) and source_range. Write to output_path via otio.adapters.write_to_file. Return output_path. Make step-5 tests pass.; Write failing tests for multi-track support: given an EDL with clips on track 1 and track 2, export_otio creates separate video tracks. Verify: timeline has 2 video tracks, each track contains only its clips, clips are ordered by in_timecode within each track.; Extend export_otio to group clips by track number and create separate otio.schema.Track for each track, sorted by in_timecode. Add gap filling between clips on same track. Make step-7 tests pass.; Write failing tests for transition mapping: EDL transition with type 'crossfade' creates an otio.schema.Transition with TransitionTypes.SMPTE_Dissolve. Test that transition duration is correctly set as RationalTime. Test 'cut' type produces no explicit transition object (it's the default).; Implement transition conversion: map EDL transition types to OTIO TransitionTypes, insert otio.schema.Transition objects between clips at appropriate positions. Make step-9 tests pass.; Write failing tests for metadata preservation: given an EDL with crop_modes, audio_settings, titles, music, voiceovers, and broll_requests, export_otio preserves all metadata. Verify: clip-level metadata (crop_mode, audio level_db) on each Clip's metadata dict, timeline-level metadata (titles, music, voiceovers, broll_requests) on Timeline's metadata dict.; Implement metadata preservation: attach per-clip metadata (crop_mode, audio_settings) to Clip.metadata['autopilot'], attach timeline-level metadata (titles, music, voiceovers, broll_requests, target_duration_seconds) to Timeline.metadata['autopilot']. Make step-11 tests pass.; Write failing tests for round-trip change detection: export_otio stores an EDL content hash in timeline metadata. detect_otio_changes(otio_path, original_edl) returns a dict with 'modified' bool and 'changes' list. Test: unmodified .otio returns modified=False. Manually alter the .otio (change a clip's source_range) and detect_otio_changes returns modified=True with descriptive changes list.; Implement detect_otio_changes: compute JSON-stable hash of EDL, store as timeline.metadata['autopilot']['edl_hash']. detect_otio_changes reads .otio, compares stored hash vs hash of original_edl. Also compare clip count, track count for descriptive change list. Make step-13 tests pass.; Write failing tests for error handling: export_otio raises OtioExportError for empty clips list, missing db.get_media (clip not in catalog), invalid output_path (unwritable directory). Test detect_otio_changes raises OtioExportError for nonexistent .otio file.; Add error handling: validate EDL has clips, handle missing media gracefully (log warning, use clip_id as fallback target_url), wrap OTIO write errors in OtioExportError, handle file-not-found in detect_otio_changes. Make step-15 tests pass.; Write integration test: seed DB with 2 media files, create a full EDL (2 clips, 1 transition, audio_settings, crop_modes, titles, music, voiceover), call export_otio, verify .otio file round-trips correctly (read back, check all clips/transitions/metadata), call detect_otio_changes and verify no changes detected, store otio_path in DB via upsert_edit_plan, verify DB has correct otio_path.; Final polish: ensure integration test passes, add proper logging, verify all edge cases handled. Clean up any remaining issues.
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues, type errors

Completed all 7 review fix steps (19-25). Fixed transition tests to use position:0 with placement assertions. Added multi-track transition isolation with track filtering in _insert_transitions. Wrapped db.get_media exceptions in OtioExportError. Wrapped all bare bracket access (crop_modes, audio_settings, clip dicts) to raise OtioExportError instead of KeyError. All 41 tests pass.
